coherence_measures:
A livello nazionale
Piani Paesaggistici Regionali (PPR)
Il Codice dei beni culturali e del paesaggio (D. Lgs. n. 42/2004 e s.m.i. “Codice Urbani”) stabilisce la priorità della pianificazione paesaggistica come strumento di tutela e di disciplina del territorio.
L’art. 135 del Codice affida alle Regioni e alle Province autonome il compito di sottoporre a specifica normativa d’uso il territorio mediante piani paesaggistici, ovvero piani urbanistico-territoriali con specifica considerazione dei valori paesaggistici, entrambi di seguito denominati: ‘piani paesaggistici’. L’elaborazione dei piani paesaggistici avviene congiuntamente tra Ministero (per i beni e le attività culturali e turismo, n.d.r.) e Regioni limitatamente ai beni paesaggistici di cui all’articolo 143, comma 1, lett. b), c) e d), nelle forme previste dal medesimo articolo 143.’  L’art. 143, comma 2, stabilisce inoltre che: ‘Le regioni, il Ministero (per i beni e le attività culturali e turismo, n.d.r.) ed il Ministero dell'ambiente e della tutela del territorio e del mare possono stipulare intese per la definizione delle modalità di elaborazione congiunta dei piani paesaggistici, …’. 
Come è noto, sulla base delle competenze attribuitegli ex lege, il Ministero dell’Ambiente esercita un’attività di vigilanza nella procedura di approvazione e adozione degli strumenti di pianificazione e di governo dei Parchi e Riserve Nazionali e delle misure di conservazione relative ai siti Natura 2000, presenti nel territorio regionale.
Tali strumenti e misure dispongono una specifica normativa d’uso del territorio finalizzata: 
-	alla “conservazione e valorizzazione del patrimonio naturale” e principalmente alla conservazione di specie animali o vegetali, di associazioni floristiche e/o forestali, di comunità biologiche, di biotopi e di equilibri ecologici, nelle aree naturali protette (art. 1 commi 1 e 3 lett. a) l. n. 394/1991);
-	al mantenimento e al ripristino, in uno stato di conservazione soddisfacente, degli habitat naturali e delle specie di fauna e flora selvatiche di interesse comunitario (Direttive 92/43/CEE Habitat e 2009/147/CE Uccelli).
In tale contesto, il Ministero dell’Ambiente nei casi in cui sia stato chiamato a partecipare alla pianificazione paesaggistica regionale attraverso l’intesa di cui all’art. 143, comma 2, sopra richiamato, ha fornito il proprio contributo al processo di co-pianificazione regionale finalizzandolo a garantire, nello spirito di armonizzazione degli strumenti di governo del territorio, l'integrazione nei piani paesaggistici regionali delle norme di tutela dei valori naturalistici previste negli strumenti di pianificazione e di governo dei Parchi e Riserve Nazionali e nelle misure di conservazione relative ai siti Natura 2000, presenti nel territorio regionale.
Nell’ambito di detta attività si sta fornendo supporto alla redazione dei Piani Paesaggistici della Regione Basilicata, Umbria, Liguria, Molise, mentre è stato approvato quello della Regione Friuli-Venezia Giulia. Nell’elaborazione di detti strumenti vengono individuati i vari elementi delle reti ecologiche, in accordo con quanto stabilito dall’art. 10 della Direttiva 92/43/CEE “Habitat”.

A livello regionale
Regione Basilicata
La progettazione di Misure di tutela e Conservazione di Rete Natura 2000 in Basilicata è stata oggetto di un unico Grande progetto: Programma Natura 2000 di Basilicata con cui sono state redatte MdC e PdG con una analisi di coerenza complessiva che ha previsto un accurato processo di validazione da parte di una Cabina di Regia di 15 Esperti e da parte dei tecnici dell’Ufficio Parchi che hanno individuato punti di conflitto tra le misure e tra le azioni dei Piani di gestione. Tale confronto ha comportato l’approvazione di norme con una maggiore coerenza tra le misure adottate e altri strumenti di pianificazione che le recepiscono es. Piani di Assestamento forestale, piani urbanistici, piani dei parchi, piano paesaggistico regionale (in corso di redazione).
Le misure sono state classificate per tematiche (foreste, agricoltura, acque interne, fauna, vegetazione, attività antropiche) e sono tutte visibili e scaricabili sul sito www.retecologicabasilicata.it
Provincia Autonoma di Bolzano
-I premi incentivanti per la cura ed il mantenimento del paesaggio e della biodiversità di ambienti ad elevato valore ecologico contribuiscono al mantenimento ed alla gestione appropriata di alcuni habitat importanti per la connettività della rete Natura 2000, essendo le superfici distribuite piú o meno uniformemente sul territorio provinciale. Questa funzione la assolvono principalmente i premi per le categorie “siepi”, “canneti”, “torbiere” e “boschi ripariali”.
-	“AlpBioNet: si tratta di un progetto europeo nel Quadro del Programma Interreg-Alpin Space (2016-2019) al quale partecipano 15 partner da tutto l’arco alpino. L’obiettivo è l‘"Integrative Alpine wildlife and habitat management for the next generation”. L’Ufficio Parchi naturali collabora come subpartner con il Parco nazionale Alti Tauri che è project partner della regione pilota “Parco nazionale Alti Tauri - Parco naturale Vedrette di Ries-Aurina”. Il tema trattato in questa regione pilota è l’analisi della relazione tra fruizione turistica del territorio e sua funzione ecologica, in quanto anche habitat di specie faunistiche sensibili, nonché l’analisi tra l’utilizzo, nella pratica venatoria, di munizione al piombo e le possibili ripercussioni su specie animali, quali ad esempio l’aquila reale e il gipeto.
-	Rilevamenti di habitat umidi e aridi: questo progetto ha lo scopo di rilevare e cartografare tutte le aree di pregio sul territorio provinciale che in quanto tali risultano protette dalla Legge provinciale di tutela della natura del 12 maggio 2010, n. 6. Questo contribuisce al mantenimento della connettività di questi habitat su tutto il territorio provinciale, soprattutto in quanto premette di indirizzare le valutazioni relativi a piani e progetti in maniera tale da evitare che siano eliminati o danneggiati.
Regione Friuli-Venezia Giulia
Approvazione Piano Paesaggistico Regionale (sviluppo della parte strategica: Rete ecologica regionale e locale in collaborazione con Università degli studi di Udine e Museo friulano di storia naturale e collaborazione parte statutaria del piano su delimitazioni beni paesaggistici e individuazione altri beni)
Regione Lazio
Nella normativa e programmazione regionale la tutela degli elementi del paesaggio viene considerata nei seguenti atti e documenti programmatici:
-DGR 612/2011 “Rete Europea Natura 2000: misure di conservazione da applicarsi nelle Zone di protezione Speciale (ZPS) e nelle Zone Speciali di Conservazione (ZSC). Sostituzione integrale della Deliberazione della Giunta Regionale 16 maggio 2008, n. 363, come modificata dalla Deliberazione della Giunta regionale 7 dicembre 2008, n.928”
-La Rete Ecologica Regionale del Lazio (REcoRd_Lazio) è parte integrante del Piano Regionale per le Aree Naturali Protette (PRANP) così come previsto dall'art. 7 della legge regionale 29/97 in materia di "aree naturali protette regionali". La REcoRd_Lazio è stata finalizzata alla conservazione di specie e di habitat ai sensi delle Direttive 2009/147/CE "Uccelli" e 92/43/CEE "Habitat", nonché di altre specie di interesse conservazionistico elencate in liste ufficiali. Nel 2012 è stato elaborato un aggiornamento della Rete Ecologica Regionale. Il documento contiene le modifiche apportate al precedente rapporto (2010) resesi necessarie a seguito delle verifiche di campo e dell'elaborazione, da parte dell'Arp, della nuova Carta di Uso del Suolo (CUS 2011). 
Nell’espressione dei pareri sulle valutazioni ambientali (VAS e Vinca) e nelle istruttorie relative vengono considerate entrambe le disposizioni per la conservazione degli elementi della rete (core areas, corridors etc.).
Regione Lombardia
La legge regionale 86/83 prevede, all’Art.3ter, la definizione di una Rete ecologica regionale (RER) costituita dalle aree protette e dai Siti Natura 2000 e dalle aree, con valenza ecologica, di collegamento tra le medesime che, per la loro struttura lineare e continua o il loro ruolo di collegamento ecologico, sono funzionali alla distribuzione geografica, allo scambio genetico di specie vegetali e animali e alla conservazione di popolazioni vitali. Il disegno della Rete Ecologica Regionale e del suo dettaglio a scala provinciale e comunale è stato approvato antecedentemente al periodo di rendicontazione. Nell’ambito del progetto IPGESTIRE2020 una specifica azione è dedicata alla pianificazione di interventi necessari al ripristino della connessione ecologica a garanzia della coerenza di Rete Natura 2000. 
Regione Piemonte
Con la DGR n. 52 – 1979 del 31 luglio 2015 è stata approvata la Metodologia tecnico-scientifica di riferimento per l'individuazione degli elementi della rete ecologica regionale e la sua implementazione, che rappresenta il riferimento per l’implementazione della rete ecologica sul territorio regionale al fine di identificare le aree importanti per la biodiversità che concorreranno al completamento del disegno di rete e al suo recepimento nella Carta della Natura regionale ai sensi dell’art. 3 della legge regionale n. 19/2009.
Regione Toscana
-Deliberazione di Consiglio Regionale n.1148/02 - L.R. 56/2000 - Indicazioni tecniche per l’individuazione e la pianificazione delle aree di collegamento ecologico.
-Legge Regionale n. 30/2015 art. 7 - Aree di collegamento ecologico funzionale ed elementi strutturali e funzionali della rete ecologica toscana.
-D.C.R. 37/2015 - Atto di integrazione del piano di indirizzo territoriale (PIT) con valenza di piano paesaggistico. Approvazione ai sensi dell'articolo 19 della legge regionale 10 novembre 2014, n. 65 (Norme per il governo del territorio) – Invariante II: i caratteri ecosistemici dei paesaggi
Provincia Autonome di Trento
Per la gestione della Rete Natura 2000 la Provincia autonoma di Trento si è dotata di uno specifico strumento di delega, le cosiddette Reti di riserve (L.P. 11/2007 “Governo del territorio forestale e montano, dei corsi d’acqua e delle aree protette”) (http://www.areeprotette.provincia.tn.it/reti_di_riserve_sezione/), uno strumento nuovo e innovativo, per gestire e valorizzare le aree protette esistenti in modo più efficace e con un approccio dal basso, attivato su base volontaria dai comuni in cui ricadono sistemi territoriali di particolare interesse naturale, scientifico, storico-culturale e paesaggistico. Si tratta in sostanza di una organizzazione locale di più comuni riconosciuta dall'amministrazione provinciale e finalizzata ad una gestione delegata della conservazione della natura.
Allo stato attuale in Trentino sono state istituite 10 diverse Reti di riserve. È inoltre in fase di istituzione e sarà attivata a breve l’undicesima Rete di riserve, “Brenta”. su 135 ZSC presenti in Trentino, 103 sono quelle ricomprese all’interno di una Rete di Riserve. Per il prossimo futuro l’amministrazione intende promuovere la nascita di nuove Reti di riserve, in modo da completare l’intera rete ecologica provinciale.
Regione Umbria
Il progetto RERU (Rete Ecologica Regione Umbria), finanziato con fondi D.P.C.M. Ambiente 2001, ha portato all'individuazione sul territorio regionale di entità morfo-funzionali quali: Unità regionali di connessione ecologica, Corridoi e pietre di guado, Frammenti, Matrice, Barriere antropiche, Ambiti di elevata sensibilità alla diffusione insediativa, rappresentate alla scala 1:10.000. Tale progetto, recepito con Leggi regionali (n. 11/2005 e n. 13/2009) consente ai servizi regionali e provinciali competenti in materia, già dal 2005, un controllo puntuale sulle scelte operate dai piani regolatori comunali di nuova approvazione, mediante sovrapposizione degli stessi con gli strati informativi della RERU.

achievements:
V roce 2013 byl aktualizován národní seznam evropsky významných lokalit (EVL) v návaznosti na zavedení institutu základní ochrany lokalit v zákoně č. 114/1992 Sb., o ochraně přírody a krajiny (v roce 2009) a související revizi potřebné formy ochrany EVL s ohledem na ekologické nároky jejich předmětů ochrany. Aktualizace proběhla přijetím nařízení vlády č. 318/2013 Sb., v rámci něhož došlo rovněž ke zrušení 7 EVL. Uvedené nařízení vlády nahradilo dosavadní národní seznam v podobě nařízení vlády č. 132/2005 Sb., ve znění č. 301/2007 Sb. a č. 371/2009 Sb.
V roce 2016 byl aktualizován národní seznam EVL nařízením vlády č. 73/2016 Sb. a č. 207/2016 Sb. Výsledkem bylo vyhlášení 51 nových EVL, doplnění předmětů ochrany do 70 EVL, zrušení předmětů ochrany u 39 EVL (vedlo k vyřazení 10 EVL), upravení hranice u 289 EVL a změna názvu u 22 EVL. Doplnění bylo realizováno mj. v návaznosti na bilaterální jednání k dostatečnosti národního seznamu EVL v roce 2011 v Průhonicích (ČR). Počet EVL se tak zvýšil z 1 075 na 1 112 EVL o rozloze 7 951 km2, pokrývající cca 10 % rozlohy ČR.
Ochrana EVL je zajišťována prostřednictvím základní ochrany (tato forma ochrany vyplývá pro lokality přímo ze zákona), vyhlášením zvláště chráněného území nebo smluvně. Pro všechny EVL jsou zpracovávány souhrny doporučených opatření, které stanovují optimální péči o předměty ochrany dané EVL. K datu 31. 12. 2018 má v ČR zajištěnou ochranu a plán stanovující příslušnou péči 997 EVL (z 1 112 EVL).
Za zajištění péče o EVL jsou odpovědny příslušné orgány ochrany přírody. Na péči jsou vyčleněny národní (zejm. prostřednictvím dotačních titulů, ale i v rámci státních a krajských rozpočtů) i evropské prostředky (např. Operační program Životní prostředí – zejm. Specifický cíl 4.1 zaměřený na péči o národně významná chráněná území a Program rozvoje venkova pro hospodáře - zejm. Agroenvironmentálně-klimatická opatření, Platby v rámci sítě Natura 2000). Dále jsou využívány programy LIFE, Interreg a EHP fondy.
Pro zvláště chráněné druhy, jimž hrozí vyhynutí, jsou připravovány záchranné programy. Z evropsky významných druhů mají platný záchranný program hnědásek osikový - Euphydryas maturna (2011), hořeček mnohotvarý český - Gentianella praecox subsp. bohemica (2011), hvozdík písečný český - Dianthus arenarius subsp. bohemicus (2008), matizna bahenní - Angelica palustris (2000), perlorodka říční - Margaritifera margaritifera (aktualizace 2013), sysel obecný - Spermophilus citellus (2008) a užovka stromová - Zamenis longissimus (2008), další záchranné programy jsou ve stadiu přípravy.
Pro zvláště chráněné druhy, které působí ekonomické škody a dochází ke konfliktu s lidskými zájmy, jsou připravovány programy péče. Z evropsky významných druhů mají platný program péče vydra říční - Lutra lutra (2009) a bobr evropský - Castor fiber (2013). Připravován je program péče pro vlka obecného (Canis lupus).
Pro ohrožené druhy jsou od nedávna připravovány též regionální akční plány. Z evropsky významných druhů mají regionální akční plán mihule ukrajinská - Eudontomyzon mariae (2017, potok Račinka) a prastarček dlouholistý moravský - Tephroseris longifolia subsp. moravika (2017, CHKO Bílé Karpaty), některé další regionální akční plány jsou připravovány.
Sledování stavu stanovišť je realizováno zejm. prostřednictvím systematického procesu mapování biotopů. Od roku 2007 probíhá aktualizace vrstvy mapování biotopů, která umožňuje sledovat vývoj zastoupení a kvality biotopů v čase. Je realizována ve 12 letém cyklu. Vrstva mapování biotopů je veřejně dostupná na http://mapy.nature.cz/. Doplňkovým zdrojem dat je monitoring přírodních stanovišť na trvalých monitorovacích plochách, který byl zahájen v roce 2009. Pravidelně jsou monitorovány evropsky významné druhy, pro které jsou zpracovány metodiky monitoringu. Metodiky jsou veřejně dostupné na http://www.biomonitoring.cz/ .  

V rámci výzvy programu LIFE z roku 2017 ČR uspěla s návrhem Integrovaného projektu LIFE pro soustavu Natura 2000 v České republice (LIFE17 IPE/CZ/000005), který bude realizován v letech 2019-2026.  Díky projektu bude mj. posílena implementace směrnice o stanovištích prostřednictvím sledování stavu předmětů ochrany jednotlivých lokalit, péči o území a vyhodnocení dopadu péče s následnou aktualizací plánovacích dokumentů. Bude vyhodnocena efektivita inovativní péče v podobě pařezení dubohabřin a pastvy v lese. Péče o EVL bude diskutována s vlastníky pozemků. Budou vyhodnoceny přínosy soustavy Natura 2000 pro společnost a ty budou komunikovány veřejnosti. 
Zavedený systém odpovídajícího („naturového“) posouzení byl v roce 2018 posílen přijetím vyhlášky o jeho náležitostech v souvislosti s novelou stavebního zákona a související metodiky. Od roku 2013 jsou každoročně pořádána pravidelná setkávání osob se speciálním oprávněním (autorizací) toto posouzení provádět s cílem sjednotit praxi a komunikovat odborné přístupy hodnocení.
achievements_trans:
In 2013, the CZ updated the national list of SCIs in order to reflect the introduction of the “basal protection“  of SCIs into the Act No. 114/1992 Coll., on Nature and Landscape Protection (in 2009) and related reassessment of appropriate form of protection of each SCI with respect to the ecological requirements of its target features. The update was carried out by the adoption of the Government Decree No. 318/2013 Coll. (in addition 7 SCIs were deleted). Previous Government Decree No. 132/2005 Coll., as amended by Act No. 301/2007 Coll., and No. 371/2009 Coll. was replaced.
In 2016, the CZ updated national list of SCIs by Government Decree No. 73/2016 Coll. and No. 207/2016 Coll. As a result 51 new SCIs were designated, new target features were added into 70 SCIs, 39 target features were deleted in SCIs (leading to deletion of 10 SCIs), borders were changed in 289 SCIs and 22 SCIs were renamed. The update was implemented among others based on the results from bilateral meeting on the sufficiency of national list of SCIs held in Průhonice (CZ) in 2011. The number of SCIs increased from 1 075 to 1 112, covering an area of 7 951 km2, 10 % of the CZ area.
The CZ ensures protection of SCIs through basal protection (defined in the law), designation of specially protected areas or by contract. For each SCI, the CZ prepares a Set of Conservation Measures that defines optimal management practice for the target features. By 31. 12. 2018 the CZ has 997 SACs (from 1 112 SCIs) with ensured legal protection and a management plan.
The CZ has appointed nature protection authorities responsible for securing the management of SCIs. For the site management, the CZ has dedicated national funds (mainly through subsidy schemes but also from state and regional budgets) and European funds (e.g. Operational Programme Environment – esp. Specific Objective 4.1 focused on management of nationally important protected areas, and Rural Development Programme for land-users - esp. Agri-environment-climate measures, Natura 2000 payments). Additionally, LIFE programme, Interreg and EEA grants are used.
For selected specially protected species that are in danger of extincion, the CZ prepares Action plans. Among the Species of European Importance covered by Action plans are: Euphydryas maturna (2011), Gentianella praecox subsp. bohemica (2011), Dianthus arenarius subsp. bohemicus (2008), Angelica palustris (2000), Margaritifera margaritifera (update 2013), Spermophilus citellus (2008) and Zamenis longissimus (2008) with more in preparation.
For selected specially protected species that cause economic damage and are in conflict with human interests, the CZ prepares Management plans. Among the Species of European Importance covered by Management plans are Lutra lutra (2009) and Castor fiber (2013). A Management plan for wolf (Canis lupus) is in the process of preparation.
For selected endangered species the CZ started recently to prepare Regional action plans. Among the Species of European Importance covered by Regional action plans are Eudontomyzon mariae (2017, Račinka stream) and Tephroseris longifolia subsp. moravika (2017, Bílé Karpaty), some other are in preparation.
Surveillance of habitat types is implemented esp. through comprehensive process of habitat mapping. Since 2007, habitat mapping layer, which enables to monitor the development of occurrence and quality of habitats over time, is being updated. The update is implemented in the 12 year cycle. The layer of habitat mapping is publicly available at http://mapy.nature.cz/ . Additional source of information is monitoring of habitat types on permanent plots introduced in 2009. The CZ regularly monitors the Species of European Importance. For each species, a monitoring scheme was established and is available on http://www.biomonitoring.cz/ .    
The CZ has succeeded in the LIFE program call for 2017 with a proposal of Integrated LIFE project for the Natura 2000 network in the Czech Republic (LIFE17 IPE/CZ/000005) to be carried out through 2019-2026. The project will inter alia strengthen implementation of the Habitats Directive by assessment of target features status on sites, management of sites, assessment of the impacts of the management and subsequent update of management plans. The CZ will evaluate the effectiveness of innovative management such as oak-hornbeam coppicing management and grazing in forests. The management practices in SCIs will be discussed with land users. The benefits of the Natura 2000 network for society will be assessed and communicated to the wider public.
A well-established systém of the appropriate assessment (AA) was enhanced by the adoption of a regulation No. 142/2018 Coll. On the AA content and related Guidance in connection to Building Act amendment. Annual meetings of AA licensed experts are being held from 2013 with the aim to unify professional opinions and communicate expert approaches to conducting the assessment.

coherence_measures:
There are various aspects of ensuring the Art. 10 of the HD implementation in the CZ, both through legislative tools as well as using subsidy mechanisms.
The main purpose of the Territorial System of Ecological Stability of the Landscape (TSES) as set out by the Act No. 114/1992 Coll., on the Nature and Landscape Protection, is to support ecological stability in the landscape by conservation or restoration of ecosystems and their connectivity. The TSES consists of bio-centres, bio-corridors and interacting elements. The TSES layer is one of the analytical documents for urban planning.
The specially protected areas as set out by the Act on the Nature and Landscape protection ((national) natural monument, (national) natural reserves, landscape protected area, national park) largely overlap with Natura 2000 sites, nevertheless, they are a vital tool to ensure conservation of natural and semi-natural habitats and species habitats as ecologically valuable landscape features also outside the Natura 2000 network. They cover almost 17 % of the CZ area. 
Additionally, important landscape components as set out by the Act on the Nature and Landscape protection are environmentally, geo-morphologically or aesthetically valuable parts of the landscape that create typical appearance of landscape, or contribute towards its stability. They are protected against damage. The law includes two types of components: obligatory (forests, peat-lands, water courses, ponds, lakes and floodplain) and registered (other landscape components registered by nature conservation authorities particularly wetlands, steppe grasslands, valuable greenery in settlements formations, etc.).
The CZ has implemented project financed from EEA grants “Complex Approach to the Protection of Fauna of Terrestrial Ecosystems from Landscape Fragmentation in the Czech Republic” (2015-2017). The project prepared methodology for the protection of landscape connectivity for the key relevant groups of terrestrial animals (e.g. Lynx lynx, Ursus arctos, Canis lupus). It identified biotopes of the species and defined core areas, migration biotopes and critical barrier sites. The main output is one polygon layer, which is planned to be incorporated into obligatory urban planning processes. Similarly a layer of sites with the occurrence of specially protected species of national importance serves as an underlying analytical document for urban planning. The aim of the layer is to protect endangered species with significant occurrence outside the protected areas.
The CZ has implemented project financed from EEA grants “Development of strategy for mitigation of river fragmentation impacts in the hydrological network of the Czech Republic” (2015-2017). Current situation of watercourses fragmentation was evaluated, the streams were classified according to the urgency for solution and a schedule for implementation was set up. The information about barriers and watercourses was integrated into a database. Http://vodnitoky.ochranaprirody.cz/

achievements:
През периода 2013 – 2018 г. продължи развитието и допълването на екологичната мрежа Натура 2000 основно в морската й част. Приети са общо 5 нови защитени зони за местообитанията и са разширени границите на още 8 зони. С Решение на Министерския съвет  № 660/01.11.2013 г.  морската част от мрежата в частта и от защитени зони за местообитанията беше разширена, като се одобриха 3 нови изцяло морски зони и беше разширена акваторията на 6 от съществуващите зони. С Решение на Министерския съвет  № 223/24.04.2014 г. още една териториална защитена зона за местообитанията беше одобрена и беше разширен териториалния обхват на още две зони. С Протокол № 23 от проведено на 07.12.2018 г. заседание на Националния съвет по биологично разнообразие към министъра на околната среда и водите беше одобрена 1 нова защитена зона за местообитанията в района на Рила планина и предложена за приемане от Министерския съвет (МС) на Република България - BG0000636 „Ниска Рила“. В последствие зоната бе официално приета от МС и предложена за одобрение на Европейската комисия.

През периода на докладване бе допълнена и мрежата от защитени зони по Директивата за птиците като бяха определени 2 нови защитени зони и разширена 1 зона за опазване на дивите птици. 

Така обхвата на мрежата от защитени зони съгласно Директивата за местообитанията покрива 30.3% от територията на страната, като цялата мрежа Натура 2000 в България включва общо 341 защитени зони съгласно Директивата за местообитанията и Директивата за птиците и заема 34.9% от националната територия.

Бяха издадени и първите заповеди за обявяване на специални консервационни зони за едни от най-чувствителните зони, включващи редица административни природозащитни мерки.

В Закона за биологичното разнообразие (чл. 19) е предвидена възможността в случай на наличие на опасност от увреждане на защитени зони, преди да бъдат издадени заповедите за обявяването им, съдържащи режими или ограничения на дейности, от министъра на околната среда и водите да бъдат издавани временни заповеди за забрана или ограничаване на отделни действия в границите на зоните. Срокът на тези заповеди е до две години. През докладвания период неколкократно бяха издавани такива заповеди. 

Бяха направени промени и допълнения в нормативната уредба, насочени към подобряване контрола и управлението на пасищата, както и по-пълното интегриране на изискванията на Директивата за местообитанията в горскостопанските дейности. В резултат от обществените обсъждания в хода на обявяване на първите специални консервационни зони бе взето решение и с нарочна заповед на министъра на земеделието, храните и горите през 2016 г. бяха определени горите във фаза на старост в държавните горски територии, управлявани от Министерство на земеделието, храните и горите, попадащи в защитени зони от Натура 2000, като елемент от системата за поддържане на благоприятното природозащитно състояние на горските типове природни местообитания, съгласно изискванията на Директива 92/43/ЕИО за опазване на природните местообитания и на дивата флора и фауна. Общата площ на определените гори във фаза на старост е 109 000 ха, като определянето е направено на ниво защитена зона, при заложен минимум от 10 % гори във фаза на старост във всяка зона. 

През докладвания период бяха отчетени някои недостатъци в националната правна уредба и нейното прилагане, свързани с ефективността на управлението на Натура 2000 мрежата в страната и водещи до забавяне на процеса на обявяване на специалните консервационни зони. Във връзка с това основните усилия на администрацията през периода 2013 -2018 г. бяха насочени към отстраняването на тези недостатъци и въвеждането на нов, ефективен подход за управление на екологичната мрежа Натура 2000. Новият подход следва да оптимизира управлението на мрежата в България, както и да спомогне в най-пълна степен за постигане на целите на Директивата за местообитанията.

Новият подход бе разработен в рамките на успешно изпълнен проект, финансиран по Оперативна програма „Околна среда 2014 -2020 г.“ - „Управленски подход за мрежата „Натура 2000“. Подходът е съобразен с последните тенденции в управление на мрежата Натура 2000 в ЕС и насоките на ЕК, като е изготвен на базата на редица анализи, проучвания и предложения за решения, обсъдени широко с представителите на основните заинтересовани страни в страната в рамките на работни групи и кръгли маси. Предвижда се изграждане на допълнителен капацитет, децентрализация на процеса и въвличане на местните общности в процеса на вземане на решения.

Подходът включва въвеждане на двустепенен модел на управление на Натура 2000, включващ управление на национално и регионално ниво, създаване на Национален консултативен съвет за Натура 2000 към националния орган за управление на мрежата и Комитети на заинтересованите страни към регионалните органи, въвеждане на задължително разработване на планове за управление за всички защитени зони и четиристепенен подход за определяне на природозащитните цели, включващ разработването на национален документ за целите на Натура 2000. Четиристепенният подход е научен подход, който поставя във фокуса на управлението конкретните видове и природни местообитания, като се отчита тяхното природозащитно състояние и приносът на конкретните територии за подобряването му на биогеографско ниво.

Внедряването на новия подход за управление на Натура 2000 изисква законодателни промени, като във връзка с това в края на докладвания период стартира процедура за изменение и допълнение на Закона за биологичното разнообразие, с цел въвеждане на посочената по-горе концепция. Към настоящия момент законопроектът е внесен за съгласуване от Министерският съвет. Стартира и разработването на предвидения съгласно подхода Документ за целите на Натура 2000.

През периода 2013 – 2018 година продължи прилагането на мерките от договорен характер за поддържане и възстановяване на видовете и местообитанията, предмет на опазване съгласно Директивата за местообитанията, свързани с ползването на земеделските земи – мярка 213 – „Плащания по Натура 2000“ и мярка 214 „Агроекологични плащания“от „Програмата за развитие на селските райони за периода 2007 - 2013 г. “ (ПРСР), преадаптирани съответно в мярка 12 и мярка 10 от ПРСР 2014-2020 г.   

Също така бяха изпълнени и договорирани (все още в процес на изпълнение) редица проекти включващи директно насочени към Натура 2000 мерки, мерки за опазване и възстановяване на видове и местообитания от Директивата за местообитанията, мерки от планове за управление и планове за действие за видове, както и такива свързани с провеждането на проучвания, развитие на Националната система за мониторинг на биологичното разнообразие и популяризирането на опазването на природата и Натура 2000 мрежата. Пълни списъци и информация за възложените проекти са налични на интернет страниците на съответните финансиращи програми. По отношение на структурните инструменти на ЕС информация за всички проекти може да бъде намерена на страницата на специализираната информационна система, както следва:
-	за изпълнените проекти с финансиране от предходния програмен период (2007 – 2013 г) -  http://umispublic.government.bg/prProcedureProjectsInfo.aspx?op=-1&proc=-2&clear=1  
-	за проектите с финансиране от настоящия програмен период (2014 – 2020 г.) – http://2020.eufunds.bg/en ;  http://2020.eufunds.bg/en/0/0/Operations?ProgrammeId=AYfIOSQIKPc%3D&ShowRes=True

Освен включените в т. 7 от настоящия доклад реинтродукции на видове от Приложение 4 на Директивата, през периода са изпълнени и редица проекти за възстановяване на типове природни местообитания от Приложение 1 на Директивата и за възстановяване на популации на видове от Приложение 2 на Директивата, които не са включени в Приложение 4. В резултат на успешно приключили такива проекти са нанесени и промени в стандартните формуляри на защитените зони по отношение на местообитания 91Е0 и 9180 и видовете Балкански щипок (Sabanejewia aurata) и Главоч (Cottus gobio).

През периода на докладване бяха разработени два плана за действие за видове от приложенията на Директивата за местообитанията, които към настоящия момент са в процес на приемане – за Вълка (Canis lupus) и за Европейския лалугер (Spermophilus citellus). Бе възложено разработването на план за действие за Северния гребенест тритон (Triturus cristatus) и бяха утвърдени задания за разработване на планове за действие на още 15 животински и 5 растителни вида от Приложения 2 и 4 на Директивата.

В изпълнение на действащите през периода планове за действие за видове бяха изпълнени редица мерки, като например тези свързани с опазването на Кафявата мечка (Ursus arctos) и за намаляване на конфликта човек-мечка. Изплатените през периода обезщетения за щети, нанесени от кафява мечка, възлизат на ок. 170 000 евро. Закупени са и са раздадени на фермерите в проблемните райони електропастири и овчарски кучета. Във всички проблемни райони са сформирани и функционират групи за бързо реагиране. Проведени са обучителни кампании. Видът е обект на регулярен мониторинг.

Беше изготвена и утвърдена Инструкция за взаимодействие за повишаване на ефективността на действията при случаи на изхвърлени бедстващи живи и останки от мъртви китоподобни по Българското черноморско крайбрежие (публикувана на интернет страницата на министерството: https://www.moew.government.bg/bg/priroda/zakonodatelstvo/nacionalno-zakonodatelstvo/naredbi-pravilnici-i-instrukcii/). Към инструкцията е приложен унифициран протокол за събиране на данни. Целта е създаване на единен, ясен и работещ модел на действие при подобни случаи.
achievements_trans:
During the period 2013 - 2018, the development and expansion of the Natura 2000 network continued mainly in its marine part. A total of 5 new SCIs have been adopted and the boundaries of 8 more have been extended. By Council of Ministers Decision No. 660 / 01.11.2013 the marine part of the SCI network was expanded - 3 new entirely marine sites were added and marine parts of 6 SCIs were expanded. By Council of Ministers Decision No 223 / 24.04.2014 one more terrestrial site was approved and the boundaries of 2 SCIs were extended. By Protocol No. 23 on 07.12.2018 the National Council on Biological Diversity to the Minister of Environment and Water approved 1 new pSCI in the Rila Mountain region - BG0000636 „Niska Rila”. Subsequently, the site was formally approved by the Council of Ministers and proposed for adoption by the European Commission.

During the reporting period, the SPA network was also expanded, including 2 new sites and the expansion of one existing SPA.

Thus, the coverage of the network of protected sites under the Habitats Directive (pCSI and SCI) reached 30.3% of the national territory and the entire Natura 2000 network in Bulgaria includes a total of 341 sites under both Directives and covers 34.9% of the national territory.

The first SAC designation orders were issued for some of the most sensitive sites, containing a number of administrative nature conservation measures.

The Biological Diversity Act (Article 19) provides for the possibility temporary orders prohibiting or restricting certain activities within the sites to be issued by the Minister of Environment and Water if there is a risk of harm to the sites before their designation orders, containing prohibitions or restrictions on activities, are issued. The duration of these orders is up to two years. During the reporting period, such orders were issued several times.

Amendments and supplements to the Bulgarian legislation were made aimed at improving the control and management of grasslands, as well as more comprehensive integration of the requirements of the Habitats Directive into forestry activities. As a result of public hearings, in the course of the designation of the first SACs, a decision was made and, by a special order of the Minister of Agriculture, Food and Forestry in 2016, the old growth forests (OGF) in the state forest territories managed by the MAFF and falling within Natura 2000 sites were defined as part of the system for maintaining the favorable conservation status of the forest habitat types, in accordance with the requirements of Council Directive 92/43/EEC on the conservation of natural habitats and of wild fauna and flora. The total area of the forests assigned as OGF is 109 000 ha and the assignment was made at site level with a set minimum of 10% OGF per Natura 2000 site.

During the reporting period, some shortcomings in the national legal framework and its implementation were noted, related to the efficiency of the management of the Natura 2000 network in the country and leading to delay in the SAC designation process. In this regard, the main efforts of the administration in 2013-2018 were aimed at eliminating these deficiencies and establishing a new, effective approach to the Natura 2000 management. The new approach should optimize the management of the network in Bulgaria, and contribute to the fullest extent for the achievement of the objectives of the Habitats Directive.

The new approach was developed under the successfully implemented project “Management Approach for Natura 2000”, funded by Operational Program Environment 2014-2020. The approach is in line with the latest trends in the management of the Natura 2000 network in the EU and EC guidelines and notes. It is based on a series of analyzes, studies and proposals for solutions, widely discussed with key stakeholders in the country at working groups and roundtables. Additional capacity building, decentralization of the process and involvement of local communities in the decision-making process are envisaged in the approach.

It introduces a two-tier Natura 2000 governance model, including national and regional governance, the establishment of a National Natura 2000 Advisory Board to the National Network Management Authority and Stakeholder Committees to the regional authorities. It also envisages mandatory elaboration of management plans for all Natura 2000 sites and a four-level approach to setting conservation objectives, including the elaboration of a national document for the Natura 2000 objectives. The four-level approach is a scientific approach that puts the focus on managing specific species and habitat types, taking into account their conservation status and the contribution of the specific areas to its improvement on biogeographical level.

The introduction of the new Natura 2000 management approach requires changes in the legislation and in that regard a procedure for amending and supplementing the Biological Diversity Act was initiated at the end of the reporting period. To date, the bill has been submitted for approval by the Council of Ministers. The elaboration of the national document for the Natura 2000 objectives, envisaged by the new approach, has also started.

In the 2013 - 2018 period, the implementation of the contractual measures for the maintenance and restoration of the Habitats Directive species and habitats dependent on agricultural land use, continued - measure 213 “Natura 2000 payments” and measure 214 " Agri-environmental payments" from the Rural Development Program 2007-2013 (RDP), transferred into measure 12 and measure 10 of RDP 2014-2020 respectively.

A number of projects have also been implemented and contracted (still under implementation) including measures directly targeting Natura 2000, conservation and restoration measures for species and habitats under the Habitats Directive, measures from management plans and species action plans, as well as projects for research, development of the National Biodiversity Monitoring System and the promotion of nature conservation and the Natura 2000 network. Full lists and information of the projects are available on the websites of the respective funding programmes. Information on all projects funded by the EU structural instruments can be found in the Unified Management Information System for the EU funds in Bulgaria on the following links:
- EU co-funded projects during the 2007-2013 programming period - http://umispublic.government.bg/prProcedureProjectsInfo.aspx?op=-1&proc=-2&clear=1 
- EU co-funded projects during the 2014-2020 programming period - http://2020.eufunds.bg/en ; http://2020.eufunds.bg/en/0/0/Operations?ProgrammeId=AYfIOSQIKPc%3D&ShowRes=True

In addition to the reintroductions of Annex IV species described in section 7 of this report, a number of projects for restoration of Annex I habitats and Annex II species populations (not included in Annex IV) have been implemented during the period. As a result of the successfull completion of such projects, changes to the Standard Data Forms of several SCIs have been made concerning habitat types 91E0 and 9180 and the species Sabanejewia aurata and Cottus gobio.

During the reporting period, two action plans were elaboated for species in the Annexes to the Habitats Directive that are currently in the process of approval - for the Wolf (Canis lupus) and for the European ground squirrel (Spermophilus citellus). The development of action plan for the Crested newt (Triturus cristatus) was commissioned and terms of reference for the development of action plans for 15 other animal and 5 plant species listed in Annex II and Annex IV of the Directive were approved.

In fulfillment of the species action plans in force during the period a number of measures have been applied as, for example, those for the conservation of the Brown Bear (Ursus arctos) and the mitigation of the human-bear conflict. The amount of the paid compensations by the state budjet for damages caused by brown bears during the period is approx. € 170,000. Shepherd dogs and electric fences were bought and distributed to farmers in problematic areas. Emergency groups have been set up and operate in all problematic areas. Training campaigns have been carried out. The species is subject to regular monitoring.

An Instruction on Cooperation in Case of Cetaceans Strandings Along the Bulgarian Black Sea Coastline, was prepared and approved (published on the Ministry's website - https://www.moew.government.bg/en/nature/legislation/national-legislation/orders-ordinances-and-instructions/ ). The instruction is accompanied by a uniform data collection protocol. The aim is to create a single, clear and working model of action in such cases.

coherence_measures:
Понастоящем България е транспонирала изискванията на чл. 10 на Директива 92/43/ЕИО в Закона за биологичното разнообразие и по-конкретно в чл.30, ал.2 на същия, както и чрез чл.2, ал.2 на Наредбата за условията и реда за извършване на оценка на съвместимостта на планове, програми, проекти и инвестиционни предложения с предмета и целите на опазване на защитените зони. Всички планове свързани с устройство на територията се подлагат на оценка за съвместимост. В хода на тази оценка се цели осигуряването на необходимите взаимовръзки между елементите на мрежата „Натура 2000“, чрез осигуряване на естествено срещащи се и/или възстановени ландшафтни елементи, които трябва да служат като коридори между защитените зони. 
Отделно в редица административни и нормативни актове, както и в други стратегически документи, са разписани специфични консервационни мерки с хоризонтален характер, които имат отношение, както към поддържане и възстановяване на някои от основните елементи на ландшафта в защитените зони, така и извън тях, което има отношение и към подобряване на екологичната кохерентност на „Натура 2000“. Документи, в които са разписани подобни хоризонтални консервационни мерки са:
- Регионални планове за управление на речните басейни (ПУРБ), в които са предвидени редица мерки за опазване на реките като биокоридори. Територията на страната е изцяло обхваната от 4 такива плана за четирите Басейнови  дирекции, като в рамките на периода на докладване се изпълняваха  плановете с период на действие 2010 – 2015 г. и бяха разработени и утвърдени нови планове, с период на действие 2016-2021 г. Повече информация е достъпна на адрес https://www.moew.government.bg/bg/vodi/planove-za-upravlenie/planove-za-upravlenie-na-rechnite-basejni-purb/ , както и на съответните страници на басейновите дирекции.
- Регионални планове за управление на риска от наводненията (ПУРН).  В рамките на периода на докладване територията на страната е изцяло обхваната от 4 такива плана с период на действие 2016-2021 г. Повече информация е достъпна на адрес  https://www.moew.government.bg/bg/vodi/planove-za-upravlenie/planove-za-upravlenie-na-riska-ot-navodneniya-purn/planove-za-upravlenie-na-riska-ot-navodneniya-2022-2027vmchvch/. 
- Горскостопански планове и програми, които се изготвят във връзка с изискванията на чл. 9 и чл. 13 от Закона за горите. В тях се планират всички горскостопански дейности и въобще насоките за постигане целите на управлението на горските територии в страната за период от 10 години, в т.ч. консервационни мерки в горите, попадащи, както в Натура 2000, така и извън нея.
- В много от стратегиите за развитие на областите в България за периода 2014-2020 г. са поставени цели за възстановяване, подобряване състоянието и изграждане на нови ландшафтни елементи, което е пряко или косвено свързано с подобряване на  екологичната кохерентност на „Натура 2000“ (например рекултивация на нарушени терени в резултат на добив на инертни материали и др.). 
- Национални стандарти за добро земеделско и екологично състояние  (http://www.mzh.government.bg/mzh/ShortLinks/DZEU/NacionalniStandarti.aspx). С тях се определят правила, които следва да се спазват при упражняване на дейности в земеделските земи  с положително отражение по отношение на ландшафтните елементи на съществуващата зелена инфраструктура в земеделски стопанства. Така напр. национален стандарт 1 забранява използването на минерални и органични азотсъдържащи торове в буферните ивици около реки, потоци, канали, езера, язовири, море. Със стандарт 6 се забранява изгарянето на стърнищата, а съгласно национален стандарт 7 е задължително да се запазват и поддържат съществуващите полски граници (синори - вдлъбнати, изпъкнали или наклонени части на земната повърхност, покрити с камъни, тревни, храстови или дървесни съобщества),  трайни тераси в блока на земеделското стопанство и/или земеделския парцел, както и да се запазват и поддържат съществуващите постоянни пасища, мери и ливади от навлизането на нежелана растителност от навлизане на агресивни/инвазивни видове.
- С отношение към устойчивото управление на ландшафтните елементи на територията на страната е и разработения  през 2014 г. и утвърден със заповед на Министъра на земеделието, храните и горите слой „Постоянно затревени площи“. Той е създаден съгласно разпоредбите на чл.16д от Наредба № 105 от 22.08.2006 г. за условията и реда за създаване, поддържане, достъп и ползване на Интегрираната система за администриране и контрол. Слоят е създаден с цел подпомогне опазването на съществуващи постоянно затревени площи от разораване и преобразуване, което е и изискване на общата селскостопанска политика на ЕС през настоящия период, част от "екологизирането" на земеделието, както и запазване съотношението на заявени за подпомагане постоянно затревени площи спрямо общата заявена площ през отделните кампании за директни плащания, на национално ниво.
През периода на докладване са изпълнени и отделни проекти както за проучване на основните биокоридори, използвони от видовете, предмет на опазване от Директивата, така и за възстановяване на местообитания извън мрежата от защитени зони Натура 2000 и за създаване на „стъпкови биокоридори“ извън зоните.

achievements:
Maintaining Biodiversity

On 12 December 2012, Malta, as a Party to the CBD and as an EU Member State, adopted the National Biodiversity Strategy and Action Plan (NBSAP) entitled Working Hand-in-Hand with Nature, in line with the CBD’s Global Biodiversity Strategic Plan and the EU Biodiversity Strategy to 2020. During the reporting period, Malta has actively worked towards the implementation of the measures within the NBSAP. An assessment of the extent to which Malta’s NBSAP has been implemented measure-by-measure is provided in Malta’s 5th National Report to the Convention on Biological Diversity (2014). Out of 80 measures, excellent progress was reported for 14 measures (18%), good progress was reported for 47 measures (58%) while inadequate/ insufficient/limited progress was reported for 5 measures (6%). Progress was deemed not applicable for 14 measures (18%) for which the timeline for implementation was yet to commence. An updated assessment will be available with the upcoming publication of Malta’s 6th National Report. It is noted that during the reporting period the then competent authority for environment and development, passed through a demerger, in 2016. This created separate entities, responsible for the environment and development respectively. With the creation of the Environment and Resources Authority (ERA) Malta now has a dedicated national regulator for the environment. 

Conservation of Natural Habitats and Habitats of Species

In the current reporting cycle, efforts have continued in line with the national requirements and the NBSAP through the designation of additional marine protected areas. In fact, through the work undertaken by two related LIFE+ projects, namely LIFE+ Malta Seabird Project and LIFE+ Migrate, Malta designated additional protected areas in the marine environment. This increased the coverage of the Marine Protected Area Network from 190.96km2 to 3,487km2, in 2016. The LIFE BaĦAR for Natura 2000 project, which was finalised in 2018, further contributed to work on the designation of marine protected areas. The outcomes of this project contributed to the increase of the coverage of protected marine areas to 4,138km2, equivalent to 35.5% of the Maltese waters. Further information is available at https://era.org.mt/en/Pages/Natura-2000-Malta.aspx and http://lifebahar.org.mt/. Malta has now 14 marine Natura 2000 sites (8 SCIs and 6 SPAs), and an additional 4 pSCIs. 

As of end 2011, Malta had 27 terrestrial sites (21 SAC & 6 SAC/SPA) covering 42 km2 or 13.3 % of land area.  22 Management Plans cover 19 of these sites (one site, MT24, is split on four Management Plans) and 8 are covered by a Conservation Order. Management agreements are in place with NGOs for 10 sites to tackle management measures listed in the Natura 2000 management plans specific to the particular sites.

Moreover in 2017, 30 additional Tree Protection Areas (TPAs) have been designated under the auspices of the Trees and Woodlands Protection Regulations (SL 549.123). Through the declaration of these TPAs, these trees and woodlands are being given the well-deserved protection for our and future generations. The total number of TPAs over the Maltese Islands currently is of 60, of which 48 are in Malta, 10 are in Gozo and 2 are in Comino.

Protection of Species

Activities listed under Regulations 24 and 25 of the Flora, Fauna and Natural Habitats Protection Regulations (SL 549.44), are prohibited without permission from the ERA. The applications for permission to carry out such activities involving flora and fauna species listed respectively in Schedules V (b) and VI (b) and Schedules V (a) and VI (a) are reviewed and evaluated by the Environmental Permitting Unit within the ERA. Permits are generally issued for research, education and conservation, provided that such activities will not adversely affect the conservation status of species in question. Permits are also issued for other activities linked with the rescue and rehabilitation of injured protected species. 

Reports of illegal activities received from members of the general public are also investigated by the Enforcement and Compliance Directorate within ERA.

Malta developed a Draft Dossier on wild fauna in the Maltese islands – Capture, Killing and Exploitation, with a focus on species covered by EU Habitats Directive, Multilateral Environment Agreements (MEAs) and the Flora, Fauna and Natural Habitats Protection Regulations (SL 549.44). This Dossier includes species which are threatened or could potentially be so, by deliberate and/or incidental capture and killing, and whose exploitation should be managed, if deemed necessary upon surveillance.

In 2018, ERA published the Trees and Woodlands Protection Regulations (S.L. 549.123), which ameliorate the previous regulation on the conservation of trees and woodlands, whilst affording protection to an additional 30 tree species in the Maltese Islands, protect trees in urban public open spaces and increase controls on activities and alien species that may harm trees. These Regulations propose that persons carrying out interventions on protected trees are licensed.  The obligations in the new Regulations are also supported by substantial increases in penalties, which are based on the rarity or protection status of the tree/s. 

Research and Monitoring

In relation to terrestrial land ecosystems, surveillance and monitoring was carried out both in-house and through commissioned studies. Various studies were carried out or are ongoing, and are these being integrated with the monitoring requirements of national law, other multilateral environmental agreements and the requirements of the NBSAP. Such studies include ongoing updates on various habitats such as garrigue and phrygana habitats, maquis and matorral habitats, sand dunes, trees and woodlands, as well as selected species, including selected land invertebrates included in the Annexes of the Directive (e.g. Armadillidium ghardalamensis, Brachytrupes megacephalus, Lampedusa spp., Myrmecophilus baronii, Pseudoseriscius cameroni), herpetofauna, birds and selected mammals. Additional studies are being undertaken in relation to exploited species listed in the Flora, Fauna and Natural Habitats Protection Regulations (SL 549.44), Annex V of the EU Habitats Directive and relevant multilateral environmental agreements, or subject to selected aspects of the MSFD; these include various species, including Corallium rubrum (assessed through the LIFE BaĦAR for Natura 2000 project) and Paracentrotus lividus.

With regard to terrestrial aquatic ecosystems, nine of the ten inland surface water bodies delineated for the purpose of the WFD are located within Natura2000 sites. Malta acknowledges that due to the arid to semi-arid climate regime typical of the Maltese Islands and the limited water resources (and lack of mountains and rivers), the ecological dynamics of our inland surface waters is unique, and entirely dependent on seasonal rainwater, generally drying up entirely in the dry season. Such dynamics still need to be understood and the current monitoring programme seeks a better understanding of the dynamics of such aquatic ecosystems with a view to enable Malta to work towards better informed management practices. 

The monitoring programme for the marine environment was developed in 2014 and addresses the requirements of the WFD, MSFD, the Habitats Directive and multilateral environment agreements (including the Barcelona Convention) to the extent possible at the time of development, on the basis of common elements or parameters, indicators and methodologies. The implementation of the marine monitoring programme is being sought through a combination of national funds and the use of EMFF funds (EMFF 8.3.1 – under Union Priority 6 of Malta’s EMFF Operational Programme).

In terms of marine habitats and species, work has been ongoing through both national and EU funds, focussing on relevant marine invertebrates (e.g. Gibbula nivosa, Pinna nobilis) and migratory species, like marine turtles and cetaceans. In this respect, the LIFE+ Malta SeaBirds Project focussed on seabirds, particularly Puffinus yelkouan, Calonectris diomedea and Hydrobates pelagicus melitensis; the LIFE MIGRATE addressed Caretta caretta and Tursiops truncatus; whilst LIFE BaĦAR for Natura 2000 address the marine habitats types for which additional data was required, mainly caves, reefs and sandbanks. Whilst the data on seabirds added to earlier data, with the generation on new datasets on rafting zones, the data generated for the other species and habitats provided the baseline for further monitoring, and led to the identification and designation of additional Natura 2000 sites and the setting up of relevant After-LIFE Plans. The scientific studies provided considerable information on additional migratory species, including other dolphins, whales and turtles.

Malta is currently participating in two Interreg projects: the AMAre project, under the Interreg MED Programme, which aims, inter alia, to develop shared methodologies and geospatial tools for multiple stressors assessment, coordinated environmental monitoring, multi-criteria analyses and stakeholders' engagements in MPAs; and the HARMONY project, under the framework of Interreg V-A Italia-Malta Operational Programme (2014-2020), which aims at assessing and monitoring the impact of a number of stressors operating on a number of priority marine habitats within selected MPAs in Malta and Sicily. The latter project also seeks to streamline and to harmonise as much possible ongoing monitoring efforts conducted in Sicily and in Malta related to Descriptor 2 (Non-Indigenous Species) and Descriptor 5 (Seafloor Integrity) of the MSFD, which issues are also highly relevant to the implementation of the EU Nature Directives and the IAS Regulation.

Efforts are being undertaken to improve knowledge and data collection on the fisheries sector through the implementation of Malta’s Work Plan for Data Collection (https://datacollection.jrc.ec.europa.eu/wps). The Plan is seeking the collection of data during scientific observer trips on the incidental by-catch of birds in order to estimate the level of fishing and the impact of fishing activities on seabirds. It also includes the collection of data on discards for specific métiers including drifting longlines, bottom otter trawl, purse seines targeting small pelagic fish and bottom longlines. In 2017 and 2018, Malta also conducted a pilot study to assess the share of catches from recreational fisheries in relation to commercial fisheries for selected species.

Additional research work that is being carried out relates to the issue of marine litter and plastics. This is being addressed through studies carried out by the University of Malta (UoM) with the financial support of ERA, and other actions implemented by local environmental NGOs, through formal agreements and financial support from ERA aimed at targeting impact of plastics as ingested litter in marine reptiles and mammals, and in relation to clean-ups on land and sea, also with financial support from ERA and the Ministry for Environment, Sustainable Development and Climate Change (MESDC).

The collaboration of ERA with the University of Malta has been formalised through a Service Agreement, whereby ERA is able to assesses gaps in knowledge and commissions studies, with the University. ERA also assists in lecturing programmes and sponsors annually the publication of the research works carried out by the Department of Biology of the University of Malta, which works also focus on research needs identified by the ERA.

Measures concerning the Reintroduction of Native Species and the possible Introduction of Non-native Species

In line with the provisions of the Habitats Directive, Malta has also carried out a number of control and/or eradication programmes for the removal of invasive alien species, particularly in Natura 2000 sites such as Kemmuna, Il-Magħluq tal-Baħar ta’ Marsaskala, L-Għadira s-Safra, Il-Qortin tal-Magun u l-Qortin il-Kbir (Gozo), L-Inħawi tar-Ramla tat-Torri u tal-Irdum tal-Madonna, Majjistral Park and Dwejra (Gozo). The invasive species addressed by such interventions include amongst others Agave spp. Carpobrotus spp. Arundo donax, Yucca spp.; Schinus therebintus; Malva arborea and Opuntia ficus indica. 

Regulation 28 of the Flora, Fauna and Natural Habitats Protection Regulations (SL 549.44) addresses a number of issues related to the control of alien species. These include, inter alia:
1)	the possibility to regulate the importation and/or keeping of any species of flora and fauna, if deemed that such importation and/or keeping may harm or lead to the endangering of biodiversity of Malta; and
2)	the possibility to develop eradication or control plans and related programmes for established alien species, invasive species and those alien species with a potential to become established and subsequently invasive.

The ERA is preparing a National Strategy for Preventing and Mitigating the Impact of Invasive Alien Species in the Maltese Islands, in line with the NBSAP to ensure the proper implementation of Council Regulation No. 1143/2014 on the prevention and management of the introduction and spread of invasive alien species and related implementing national legislation. 

Education and General information relating to the Objectives of the Habitats Directive

A Natura 2000 Information Campaign was carried out between 2012-2014. This entailed extensive media presence including television spots, the launch of an information audio-visual product, the website http://natura2000malta.org.mt, a Facebook page entitled “Natura2000 Malta” and various stakeholder meetings. An outdoor photographic exhibition was also carried out, in the summer of 2016, in a number of towns and villages around the Maltese Islands, portraying snippets of Malta’s marine biodiversity and its uses. A selection of the photos exhibited were exposed at the Justus Lipsius Building during Malta’s Presidency of the Council of the European Union during the first six months of 2017. Three Public Attitude Surveys (PAS) were carried throughout the information campaign, which showed that the public was overall more aware of Natura 2000 than they had previously been at the onset of the project.

The communication efforts and stakeholder involvement initiatives, as part of the ‘Public and Stakeholder Engagement Campaign’, were an integral and vital component of the Natura 2000 management planning process. These efforts were deemed as effective and successful, by the Chartered Institute of Ecology and Environmental Management (CIEEM), which awarded the Maltese Natura2000 project with the award for the ‘Best Practice Award for Stakeholder Engagement’ in 2016 (https://era.org.mt/en/Pages/Natura-2000-Project-Award.aspx).

In relation to focussed awareness activities, ERA participates in “Malta u lil Hinn Minnha”, an educational television programme broadcasted on Sundays, with various repeats throughout the week, which has been awarded numerous journalism prizes. During this programme, ERA provides a weekly guided visit to a protected area, with information snippets on Malta’s natural heritage. 

Some events were specifically organised to commemorate other global biodiversity related events such as Arbor Day, Natura 2000 Day, Biodiversity Day, Turtle Day, the International Bat Night, Earth Week and local festivals with the aim to maximise stakeholder engagement. 

ERA organised and coordinated a number of guided biodiversity tours, for the public to experience the local nature and biodiversity in various protected areas. Throughout the tours, the participants engaged in a series of hands-on activities and quizzes based on the information provided on the local biodiversity, the pressures and threats, and on the legal considerations of protected areas. In 2018, a series of four “Bat Nights” were organised during the summer months, thus ensuring a continued outreach effort by the Authority, during the period when no guided biodiversity tours are held, in view of the hot weather conditions typical of the Maltese dry months. 

Additional information on specific issues related to habitats and species and Natura 2000 were or are also covered by communication campaigns, including the national campaigns on invasive alien species, national species, marine protected areas, waste management (and aspects linked with biodiversity), green infrastructure, and related information under the aforecited LIFE projects.

March 2017 saw the launch of the interactive Mobile App “Malta Flora & Fauna”. This app offers its users a digital catalogue of flora and fauna species found on the Maltese Islands. Users can submit geo-tagged photos to obtain information on them and at the same time assist ERA to discover and record new native and alien species, and the Plant Health Directorate in recognising common pests that could attack plants and trees, and report such cases through the app. Under the ‘Places of Interest’ section of the Malta Flora & Fauna App, information is provided on a list of Maltese parks and protected areas, and work is ongoing update this section with information on the terrestrial Natura 2000 sites, trails and events. 

Following the formation of ERA, in 2016, a dedicated email address (info@era.org.mt) was set up alongside the website of the Authority, with the aim to assist and address the various environmental requests for information from the public and stakeholders. Work is also ongoing on the development of a mobile app focussed on customer care, which will have the aim to assist in reduce response time

In relation to management of visitors, visitor centres were set up in a number of areas, targeting different geographical parts of the Maltese Islands, with a number of these handled by central government, whilst others administered by eNGOs or the private sector. Some of these area target directly Natura 2000 sites, whilst others provide information on biodiversity and cultural heritage. Additional initiatives linked with visitor and access management in Natura 2000 sites have been carried out, through the provision of dedicated footpaths, cordoning and even the placing of boulders (https://www.independent.com.mt/articles/2018-09-20/local-news/ERA-places-boulders-to-stop-vehicles-from-illegally-entering-Dahlet-ix-Xmajjel-6736196595).
